red_reasons: Evidence Appendix missing while report contains evidence/citation language
Evidence Appendix missing while report contains evidence/citation language

supported_findings: post-patch A (20260724T010632Z) evidence_entries dropped to 0 from pre-patch A stability 5 (20260723T042627Z)
post-patch B (20260724T010756Z) evidence_entries dropped to 0 from pre-patch B diagnostics 5 (20260723T160538Z)

both post-patch runs continued producing 19-21 double-quoted spans (quote_candidates) — model did not stop quoting entirely, only stopped using the `Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.` recognized citation format
both post-patch runs dropped the Evidence Appendix entirely
no checker/parser/harness change occurred; therefore the change in output shape is entirely on the model-generation side
R2 terminal-punctuation-only fires dropped to 0 in both post-patch runs, consistent with zero recognized citation-format quotes rather than a checker defect
inferences_plausible: the patch added strong DO NOT clauses (paraphrase, grammar repair, ellipsis, word add/remove) plus a self-check requiring exact-substring verification with 'shorten OR replace' — 'replace' left omission as a valid safe-compliance path

pre-existing prompt language for the Appendix was permissive ('End the report with a short evidence appendix listing the JDs cited') with no MUST, no explicit heading contract, and no minimum count — the patch increased citation risk without tightening structural obligation

the top-5-gaps bullet template says 'quote a phrase if useful' and 'One evidence quote from a real JD (give the company)' — neither is worded as mandatory; combined with new negative constraints, the model chose the safest compliance path (omission)

the model appears to have interpreted 'if a quote is not exact, shorten or replace it with an exact contiguous source span' as permission to substitute a shorter double-quoted phrase in free-form prose that no longer matches the checker regex
unknowns: whether other Sonnet 4.6 samples with different temperature or system-instruction placement would exhibit the same behavior
whether the Evidence Appendix would have been dropped without the self-check clause (only the combined patch was tested)
whether adding a positive mandatory example without any negative constraints would alone suffice, versus mandatory-structure plus verbatim-contract together
whether the observed omission is stable across many runs or a probabilistic edge case that would sometimes still emit citations

healthy_report_evidence_counts.note: 5d A — rare pre-patch outlier where model omitted Appendix intermittently

recommended_minimum_evidence_count_ratio: healthy runs consistently produce 5 evidence entries because the current output has 5 numbered gap items, each with 'One evidence quote from a real JD' — N=5 matches the existing structural convention (one quote per gap) without inflating verbosity or introducing filler

structural_instructions_evidence.text: Allowed: quoting language from a specific JD in the evidence list (with the jd_id / company tag), since the user can verify it in the appendix.
Why it's a gap for THIS user (what's missing from THIS resume — quote a phrase if useful)
One evidence quote from a real JD (give the company)
structural_instructions_evidence.note: under 'For each gap' — implies 1 per gap = 5 total but never labeled MUST

structural_instructions_evidence.text: End the report with a short evidence appendix listing the JDs cited (jd_id + company + title only).
structural_instructions_evidence.note: PERMISSIVE — no MUST, no explicit heading, no format spec, easy to skip

structural_gaps: no `MUST` wording for any evidence element
no minimum count stated for `Evidence quote:` lines
no explicit `## Evidence Appendix` heading required
no prohibition against omission when exact quoting is difficult
no requirement to shorten instead of omit
no format specification for the `Evidence quote:` line as an output contract (checker regex expectation lives only in the checker)

structural_invariants.text: final report MUST include a section headed `## Evidence Appendix`
report MUST include at least 5 `Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.` lines, one per numbered gap in the top-5 gaps section
every major gap MUST cite at least one jd_id via the `Evidence quote:` line under that gap
each evidence line MUST use the exact format `Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.` (with straight double quotes, em/en dash, comma, and jd_XXXXXX suffix)
Evidence Appendix MUST list every cited jd_id from body citations, with jd_id + company + title columns; duplicates de-duplicated
no required evidence element may be omitted because an exact full sentence is unavailable — use a shorter exact fragment instead

faithfulness_invariants.text: every quoted span must be one exact contiguous substring of the cited JD body
no ellipsis (`...` or `…`) inside a quote to bridge separated source spans
no grammar repair inside quotation marks
no insertion, deletion, or replacement of words, tense, plurality, articles, or conjunctions inside quotation marks
explanatory text must remain outside quotation marks
grammatically incomplete source fragments are explicitly allowed and preferred over stitched-together spans

completion_invariants.text: before final output, verify the required 5 `Evidence quote:` lines exist
before final output, verify the `## Evidence Appendix` section exists
before final output, verify every quoted string is exact (character-for-character) in the supplied body for the cited jd_id
before final output, verify every cited jd_id exists in the supplied EVIDENCE JDs list
failure to find a long exact quote MUST lead to a shorter exact quote from the same source, not omission
omission of any required evidence element is invalid output; the self-check MUST NOT accept a report that lacks the required structure

options.summary: mandatory structure + exact quote rules + positive example + shorter-fragment fallback + final checklist framed as omission-invalid
options.impl_size: small (~40 lines of prompt text in one file)
options.failure_mode: model still probabilistically omits under stress; needs paid A/B to confirm

options.summary: prewrite required headings and evidence slots in the output skeleton (positive slot-filling)
options.parser_checker_impact: zero if skeleton uses existing Markdown shape
options.failure_mode: over-prescriptive skeleton may reduce report flexibility
options.note: the user-suggested B-lite variant is Option A + a mandatory Appendix heading + a mandatory 5-line evidence count enforcement in the skeleton — this is what the recommended design (below) actually adopts

options.summary: model emits JSON quote+interpretation+jd_id triples; server renders Markdown
options.impl_size: large (streaming JSON + server render + client update + harness)
options.reason_deferred: too large for first iteration; keep for later if A/B insufficient

options.summary: server-side structural check + automatic regeneration if Appendix or 5 quotes missing
options.failure_mode: implicit retry violates 'no retry behavior' rule locked in prior DECISIONs
options.reason_rejected: not authorized under current no-retry policy; would need separate DECISION

options.summary: model outputs (jd_id, start_index, end_index) tuples; server slices exact source text
options.impl_size: very large (model must reliably locate byte offsets in supplied JD body)
options.failure_mode: model offset accuracy unproven at scale

recommended_design.primary: Option B-lite = Option A + mandatory skeleton enforcement
recommended_design.rationale: smallest surface that fixes both prior failures: adds mandatory `## Evidence Appendix` heading + mandatory 5 `Evidence quote:` lines + shorter-fragment-over-omission fallback + positive example + verbatim contract, while preserving current Markdown, parser regex, checker, harness, R1, R2
recommended_design.why_not_pure_A: prior implement loop demonstrated pure A-style (verbatim + self-check) without mandatory structure led to omission; the fix must add positive obligation, not just negative constraints
recommended_design.why_not_C_D_E: all deferred as too large or violating current no-retry policy

draft_contract.insertion_strategy: add one CRITICAL block near existing evidence and company-specific claim instructions AND tighten existing lines 95/97/119 with MUST wording; do NOT remove any existing rule
part_1_mandatory_structure: The final report MUST include a section headed exactly `## Evidence Appendix`.
The Evidence Appendix MUST list each cited jd_id once with columns jd_id, company, title (tab or `|` separated).

The 'Your top 5 gaps' section MUST contain 5 `Evidence quote:` lines total (one per gap) in this EXACT format: `Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.` (straight double quotes, em-dash, comma, jd_XXXXXX suffix).
Omission of any required evidence element is INVALID output. Never omit an Evidence quote line because exact quoting is difficult — use a shorter exact fragment from the same JD instead.
part_2_exact_quote_rules: Text inside quotation marks must be one contiguous substring of the cited JD body, copied character-for-character.
Do not paraphrase, repair grammar, or bridge separated spans with ellipsis inside quotation marks.
Do not add, remove, or change words, tense, plurality, articles, or conjunctions inside quotation marks.
Fragmentary source text is valid inside quotes; put explanation and grammatical framing OUTSIDE the quotation marks.
part_3_shorter_fragment_fallback: If a complete sentence cannot be quoted exactly, use a shorter exact fragment from the same JD.
A 5-word exact fragment beats a 20-word paraphrase.
A 5-word exact fragment ALSO beats omission — never omit the required Evidence quote line.
part_4_final_self_check: Before finalizing, verify ALL of the following:
- 5 `Evidence quote:` lines exist (one per numbered gap)
- `## Evidence Appendix` section exists and lists each cited jd_id once
- every quoted string appears exactly (character-for-character) in the supplied body text for the cited jd_id
- every cited jd_id exists in the supplied EVIDENCE JDs list
If any of these is not satisfied, add or shorten quotes until all 4 checks pass. Do NOT publish a report that omits the required structure.

positive_example.purpose: demonstrate short-fragment acceptance and interpretation-outside-quotes shape
positive_example.example_text: Evidence quote: "agentic RAG at scale" — ExampleCo, jd_999999. This phrase shows the archetype expects hands-on production experience with retrieval-plus-tool-use, not just prompt engineering.
characteristics: short exact contiguous fragment inside quotes (3 words)
synthetic jd_id to avoid proprietary content

negative_examples.purpose: demonstrate what does NOT count as valid

examples.example: Evidence quote: "agentic RAG at scale ... hands-on production" — ExampleCo, jd_999999.
examples.why_invalid: combines two non-contiguous source spans via ellipsis

examples.example: Evidence quote: "agentic RAG working at scale" — ExampleCo, jd_999999.
examples.why_invalid: inserts 'working' to grammar-repair source phrase 'agentic RAG at scale'

examples.example: Under gap #3: [no Evidence quote line at all]
examples.why_invalid: structural mandate requires 5 total; omission is invalid

instruction_hierarchy.system_level_critical_contract: one new CRITICAL block near lines 79-80 covering both structure and exactness
instruction_hierarchy.section_level_tightening: in the 'Your top 5 gaps' bullet template (line 97), replace 'One evidence quote from a real JD (give the company)' with mandatory-labeled MUST wording that spells out the exact format
instruction_hierarchy.output_skeleton_tightening: replace line 119 with a MUST-labeled `## Evidence Appendix` heading contract
instruction_hierarchy.final_checklist_placement: at end of prompt, immediately before the closing backtick — 4-bullet mandatory checklist
instruction_hierarchy.duplication_policy: structure and exactness rules stated once in critical block; specific format restated in section template (line 97) and in checklist — controlled repetition is fine, avoid restating the DO NOT list 4 times
instruction_hierarchy.omission_prohibition_placement: must appear in the critical block AND the checklist — closes the escape path

stages: apply refined prompt in src/lib/prompts.ts (small diff)
run Fixture A exactly once (no retries)
run Fixture B exactly once (no retries)
success_criteria: Evidence Appendix present in A and B
recognized evidence_entries >= 5 in A and B
jd_000201 either correctly cited with exact contiguous quote OR legitimately not selected while other valid evidence remains
future_paid_validation_plan.jd_000201_nuance: success does NOT require jd_000201 to be cited every run; success means it must not be incorrectly quoted if selected; omission is acceptable only if the overall evidence apparatus remains healthy

Q1_mandatory_evidence_count.question: Should a minimum count of Evidence quote lines be mandatory?
Q1_mandatory_evidence_count.recommendation: YES — omission of the whole apparatus was the observed failure mode; mandatory count closes that escape path

Q2_minimum_count_value.question: What should the minimum count be?
Q2_minimum_count_value.recommendation: 5 (one per numbered gap) — matches existing 'Your top 5 gaps' structural convention and prior healthy-run norm; do not introduce filler citations by choosing 6+

Q3_mandatory_appendix.question: Should Evidence Appendix be explicitly mandatory?
Q3_mandatory_appendix.recommendation: YES with an exact `## Evidence Appendix` heading and column contract; current 'End the report with a short evidence appendix' is too permissive

Q4_omission_forbidden.question: Should omission because of quote difficulty be explicitly forbidden?
Q4_omission_forbidden.recommendation: YES — this is the critical fix; must appear in both the critical block and the final checklist

Q5_positive_example.question: Should a positive short-fragment example be included?
Q5_positive_example.recommendation: YES — one compact synthetic example showing exact short fragment inside quotes + explanation outside; anchors format by imitation and demonstrates the shorter-fragment fallback

Q6_negative_example.question: Should a negative ellipsis-bridging example be included?
Q6_negative_example.recommendation: YES — 2-3 short synthetic negative examples (ellipsis bridging, grammar repair, omission); explicit anti-patterns prevent re-emergence of prior failure

Q7_self_check_ordering.question: Should the self-check come after structural completion checks?
Q7_self_check_ordering.recommendation: YES — the checklist should verify structural presence FIRST (Appendix + 5 lines) then exactness (character-for-character match); this ordering prevents 'passed exactness by omitting everything'

Q8_future_structural_validator.question: Should a future structural validator fail zero-evidence or missing-Appendix runs?
Q8_future_structural_validator.recommendation: YES eventually, but in a separate design loop; do not couple this design to a checker-side change; prompt-only first

Q9_first_impl_prompt_only.question: Should the first implementation remain prompt-only?
Q9_first_impl_prompt_only.recommendation: YES — matches minimum-first / anti-overengineering principle; keeps checker/parser/harness untouched; enables clean revert if it fails again

Q10_paid_ab_before_promotion.question: Is another paid A/B run required before any promotion-related work?
Q10_paid_ab_before_promotion.recommendation: YES — no promotion-related work (5f-promote, baseline promotion, blocking QI) until at least one paid A+B loop demonstrates the refined prompt fixes both structure and exactness
